name: Tahnabour (Yogurt Soup)
recipeInstructions:
Tahnabour is a traditional soup served in most regions of Armenia. It is a light soup said to aid digestion and often fed to those ill with a "sick stomach."
1. In a 3-quart saucepan, combine 2 tablespoons water, 1 tablespoon flour, and the egg. Beat well with an egg beater.
2. Mix the remaining water with Madzoon (yogurt) to dilute it, then add to the egg mixture. Heat gently and stir constantly until it reaches a boil. Do not allow it to boil vigorously.
3. Add the barley and season with salt to taste. Stir in the dried mint.
4. In a separate pan, sauté the chopped onion in oil and butter until reduced and clear. Add paprika.
5. Combine the vegetable and spice mixture with the soup. Warm gently without boiling.
